title: rubia olivieri subsp. brachypoda
intro: Rubia olivieri subsp. brachypoda, also known as dwarf madder, is a subspecies of the Rubia olivieri plant, which is a perennial herb native to the Mediterranean region. The plant's root and root bark contain anthraquinones, giving it medicinal properties that have been used for centuries to treat various health issues. The health benefits of Rubia olivieri subsp. brachypoda include its ability to act as a purgative, improve digestion, and reduce inflammation, making it a valuable treatment for conditions such as constipation and arthritis. Traditionally, the plant is used in herbal preparations, including infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are taken orally to harness its medicinal properties.
